title: Pre-Republic era
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Pre-Republic period}}
{{Update|[[Star Wars: Dawn of the Jedi]]}}

{{Quote|Now, a long time ago, before there was a Galactic Republic…|[[Bedran Veb]]|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref>}}
The '''Pre-Republic Era''' (—[[25,053 BBY]]), also known as the '''Early Hyperspace Age''',<ref name="AOTC:ICS">''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref> was the period before the formation of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. It included the formation and evolution of all the [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the dawn of their civilizations and their first developments in [[Hyperspace/Legends|space travel]], and ended in approximately 25,053 BBY, when the Republic was founded.

Pre-Republic history was largely dominated by ancient and mysterious species who developed interstellar travel long before [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and other, more well-known species. Such species included the [[Columi/Legends|Columi]], [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]], and [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]]. The names and histories of some of these species have been entirely lost to time, including the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]]. In their travels about the galaxy, many of these civilizations affected the evolution and cultural development of the more primitive species that would later dominate galactic affairs. Some built massive, mysterious structures for these and other purposes, including [[Centerpoint Station]], [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]], the [[Foundry (space station)|Foundry]], and the [[Star Forge]].

==History==
{{Quote|Out of the Great Void came the universe; its galaxies, star systems, and suns. Everything that exists, and will ever exist, was spawned from the primordial plasma of time's beginning.|The [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of [[Ood Bnar]]|<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" />}}
In the beginning, there was the [[Big Bang]].<ref name="Tales of the Jedi Companion" /> Eventually, the galaxy and life on its [[Planet/Legends|planets]] came into being. For billions of years, those [[Species/Legends|species]] evolved and formed civilizations, and eventually a few took to the stars. [[Celestial/Legends|These first spacefarers]] have been forgotten, their only legacy the large artificial structures they left behind. The [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]] was an artificial creation from this era, and the [[Drall/Legends|Drall]] and [[Selonian/Legends|Selonians]] were transplanted by one of these unknown species to planets within the system.<ref name="New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The presence of certain flora and fauna on many different worlds, such as the [[Bantha/Legends|bantha]], was likely also due to the intervention of these advanced species.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope">''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]''</ref> The ancient species of the galaxy took a hand in the shaping of many worlds as well; [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] were two such planets whose biospheres were manipulated by these civilizations.<ref name="KOTOR" /> Some time after this, the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]] became one of the first identified species to develop space travel.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4">''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]''</ref> For a time little happened on a galactic scale; species such as the [[Gree (species)/Legends|Gree]] and [[Columi/Legends|Columi]] rose briefly before disappearing again.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

After the independent discovery of a [[The Force/Legends|mystical power]] on several worlds across the galaxy, a group of [[Tho Yor|mysterious vessels]] gathered [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] and brought them together to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] world [[Tython/Legends|Tython]]. In [[36,453 BBY]], the [[Je'daii Order]] was founded to codify beliefs and study about the Force.<ref name="DOTJ1" />

[[File:Rakata.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Rakata]]

The first known civilization to seize power on a large scale was the Rakatan [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]]. By [[30,000 BBY]] it reached its peak, spanning worlds as far apart as [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] and [[Honoghr/Legends|Honoghr]], although in actual number, it was composed of relatively few planets.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" /> The [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] embraced slavery to fuel their Empire, ruling tyrannically over many species,<ref name="KOTOR" /> including the [[Kumumgah]],<ref name="DOTJ1" /> [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]],<ref name="KOTOR" /> and [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="Author's Cut">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-the-celestials|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare'' Author's Cut — The Celestials|int=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut}}</ref> As a monument to their power, they built the Star Forge, an artifact of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] that powered their vast war machine.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Meanwhile, on [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], an equally important development was occurring. In [[25,793 BBY]], some of the mystics wished to use the power of the Force to increase their own stature, and the followers of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Bogan]], as they called it, soon found themselves in conflict with the followers of the Ashla, and soon Tython was engulfed in the [[Force Wars]]. By [[25,783 BBY]], the side of the Ashla won, and in the wake of the war, they formed themselves into an order of monastic knights dedicated to the principles of peace and harmony. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had been born.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" /><ref name="history" />

The Infinite Empire soon found itself stretched too far, and the aggression that powered it turned the Rakata on themselves. A slave revolt throughout the Empire, a plague that killed only Rakata, and a civil war that broke out on their homeworld of [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]] quickly sped the Empire to its collapse around [[25,200 BBY]]. Free from Rakatan oppression, the former slave species of Humans and [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] developed rapidly, adopting abandoned Rakatan technology to speed innovation along. Within 200 years, [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel made it feasible to travel quickly, cheaply, and conveniently between planets across the galaxy.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

[[File:Atlas_pre-republic.jpg|thumb|right|Civilization centers of the galaxy soon after the collapse of the Rakata]]
In [[25,130 BBY]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], the warlord [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] came to power in the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]]. He unified its governments into a highly expansionist [[Xim's empire|empire]] and set about conquering its neighbors. As he reached the [[Si'Klaata Cluster/Legends|Si'Klaata Cluster]], this brought him into conflict with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], whom he fought to a standstill at the [[First Battle of Vontor|First]] and [[Second Battle of Vontor|Second Battles of Vontor]]. The Hutts signed the [[Treaty of Vontor]] with the native species of the Si'Klaata Cluster, recruiting them to fight Xim. When they fought again at the [[Third Battle of Vontor]], Xim was finally defeated and captured.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

The species of the galaxy were ready to unite. The hyperdrive had been perfected, and the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] were freely accessible. The [[Unification Wars (Galactic Republic)|Unification Wars]] were the last major conflict before this could happen, and in their wake, the Galactic Republic was created with the signing of the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]].<ref name="New Essential Chronology" /><ref name="Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref> Thus ended the Pre-Republic era, and so began the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Expansionist]] and [[Old Republic era]].<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

==Civilization==
===Humans===
Conflicting theories on the origins of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] abound, but prominent historians believed that [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] was their most-likely homeworld. The [[Zhell/Legends|Zhell]] were the earliest-known Human civilization, composed of thirteen nations, but nothing on them remained except the poem ''[[Dha Werda Verda]]'', describing their ancient battles with the [[Taung/Legends|Taung]].<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

As Humans developed interstellar travel, they colonized many worlds, including the Core Worlds of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Axum]], [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and [[Metellos/Legends|Metellos]]. The presence of Humans on the Outer Rim world of [[Argai]] was a mark of the extent of Human colonization efforts. When the Infinite Empire arose, Corellia and Coruscant fell under their control and were forced into slavery, until the Empire's collapse.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

[[File:Xims-war-robot_negtd.png|thumb|right|200px|One of Xim's [[war-robot]]s]]

====Xim's Empire====
{{Main|Xim's empire}}
Descended from early Coruscanti colonists, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] built an [[Xim's empire|empire]] in the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] in [[25,130 BBY]], conquering and unifying the many governments of the cluster.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" /> To eliminate the enemies of his empire, he formed a secret police force, the [[GenoHaradan]], who would outlast the empire by over twenty thousand years.<ref name="KOTOR" /> Eventually, his imperialism brought him into conflict with the Hutts. He was defeated, and his empire destroyed, at the [[Third Battle of Vontor]].<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

====Azure Imperium====
The [[Azure Imperium]] was an empire of Core Worlds lying along the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]. Its capital was [[Axum]], whose sister world [[Anaxes/Legends|Anaxes]] provided the shipyards and defenses that guarded the [[Axum system]]. Hundreds of years before the Republic, the Imperium was peacefully annexed by Coruscant.<ref name="Coruscant and the Core Worlds">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref>

====Seoularians====
The [[Seoularian]]s were a Human race from [[Seoul 5]] whose civilization spanned many planets, and the ruins of whose garrisons could still be found on several planets eons later, after they had gone extinct. They were technologically advanced and had created a set of crystals capable of boosting mental energy and generating great power, enough to destroy a planet when brought together.<ref name="Seoul Searching">[[Star Wars (1977) 84|''Star Wars'' (1977) 84]]</ref>

The Seoularians were killed in a massive war, which was mistakenly believed to have destroyed their homeworld. They worshiped the mother goddess [[Onrai]], mistress of the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Notron]], their name for the homeworld of humanity. Humans, when viewing statuary of Onrai, sometimes felt a strange familiarity with her.<ref name="Seoul Searching" />

===Duros===
The [[Duros]] were early pioneers of space travel. Like Humans, they spread amongst the stars of the Core, colonizing worlds such as [[Neimoidia/Legends|Neimoidia]]. They too were enslaved by the Infinite Empire.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

[[Duro/Legends|Duro's]] government was a hereditary monarchy. The last ruler of the Duros before the foundation of the Republic was [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Rana Mas Trehalt]].<ref name="Coruscant and the Core Worlds" />

===Infinite Empire===
{{Main|Infinite Empire/Legends}}
The Rakatan Infinite Empire spanned much of the galaxy, though its worlds numbered few. The Infinite Empire was built on the backs of slaves and powered by the Star Forge. The Rakata used the Force to carve their Empire out,<ref name="KOTOR" /> as it powered their [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] technology<ref name="Author's Cut" /> and the Star Forge itself.<ref name="KOTOR" /> The Star Forge may have been what eventually blinded their species to the Force.<ref name="KOTORII">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> Then, a vicious plague that swept the Empire and killed only Rakata, and a sudden slave uprising eventually resulted in the fall of the Empire, and the Rakata retreated to their home planet of Lehon.<ref name="KOTOR" />

===Hutts===
{{Main|Hutt Empire}}
Originating on [[Varl/Legends|Varl]], the Hutts built their expansionist [[Hutt Empire]] in the Outer and Mid Rims.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 4" /> They colonized several worlds, including [[Boonta/Legends|Ko Vari]] and [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Evocar]], where they encroached on the native [[Evocii]] population and eventually became the dominant force in the galaxy for a few centuries, between the decline of the Rakata and the rise of the Republic.<ref name="Dark Empire Sourcebook">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> The Hutt victory over the forces of Xim at [[Vontor]] and their conscription of the [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinians]], [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]], and [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodran]] cemented their place as a permanent power in the region later known as [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]].<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

===Sith===
{{Main|Sith (species)/Legends}}
[[File:Sith_Purebloods_JMGD.jpg|thumb|left|240px|The ancient Sith species]]
The Sith species originated on the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Though they engaged in practices such as sentient sacrifice, held a strong caste system, and were in an almost constant state of war, their civilization was quite sophisticated; they saw these acts not as cruel or barbaric, but simply basic aspects of existence. Their social order was very rigid. Circa [[28,000 BBY]], [[Adas]], marked as a chosen one from a young age by his unusual black skin, united the nations<ref name="Evil Never Dies">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> and [[Circle (Sith Pureblood)|tribes]]<ref name="Old Republic Encyclopedia">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> of Korriban in violent warfare to become the first [[Sith King]]. He took the title of ''[[Sith'ari/Legends|Sith'ari]]'', meaning "overlord," and was seen by his people as an immortal being who would rule forever.<ref name="Evil Never Dies" />

Eventually the Rakata arrived on Korriban. Pretending to ally themselves with Adas at first and giving him the technology of [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]], they soon revealed their intentions and attempted to conquer the Sith. Adas and the Sith fought back and drove off the forces of the Infinite Empire, with the king giving his life in the process. Without Adas's strong hand, Korriban soon collapsed into [[Korriban civil war|civil war]], with many naming themselves ''Sith'ari''. The massive infighting eventually forced the Sith to relocate their capital to [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]].<ref name="Evil Never Dies" />

===Columi===
Around [[100,000 BBY/Legends|100,000 BBY]], the [[Columi/Legends|Columi]] left their homeworld of [[Columus/Legends|Columus]] and surveyed the galaxy, observing in particular the civilizations of Coruscant and [[Duro/Legends|Duro]]. Finding nothing of interest, they returned to Columus.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

[[File:KwaStarTemple-SWREP24.jpg|thumb|right|150px|The Kwa civilization built the [[Star Temple]]s]]

===Gree===
In the Outer Rim, the [[Gree (species)/Legends|Gree]] built their own interstellar civilization. Gree society was centered on innovation, with all principal social castes except administrators involved in the design, creation, and use of new inventions. The Gree created several marvels of technology, many of whose secrets were lost to time. The Gree civilization, like many other such ancient civilizations, eventually decayed into a shadow of its former glory.<ref name="The Gree Enclave">{{JournalCite|8|The Gree Enclave}}</ref>

===Kwa===
A reptilian species, the [[Kwa/Legends|Kwa]], lived on [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] and built the [[Infinity Gate]]s that allowed them to teleport between worlds. Eventually the Kwa civilization collapsed, nearly exterminated by the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]].<ref name="Infinity's End">''[[Star Wars: Infinity's End]]''</ref><ref name="TEA" />

===Sharu===
The [[Sharu/Legends|Sharu]], a humanoid race native to the [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa system]], constructed colossal plastic pyramids and the [[Mindharp of Sharu/Legends|Mindharp]] of Sharu, a strange object that constantly changed form as it shifted through dimensions. During this time, the Sharu developed an enormous fear of something, believed to be the Celestials, and hid underground, using mind altering life orchards to drain their intelligence and becoming the primitive Toka.<ref name="Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Mindharp of Sharu]]''</ref><ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

===Killiks===
[[File:Killikmounds.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Killik mounds]]

Originating on Alderaan, the insectoid [[Killik/Legends|Killik]] species developed space travel and eventually expanded to the nearby world of Alsakan. In approximately [[35,000 BBY]] the species was driven from the Core into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] by the [[Celestial/Legends|Celestials]].<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Alien Species">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>

When Human colonists arrived on these worlds millennia later, the only signs of prior civilization were the massive mounds they had left behind, including the [[Castle Lands]] of Alderaan.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

==Technology==
With most worlds isolated from each other, the technological history of the Pre-Republic era largely consisted of the typical developments required to form an industrial civilization. Worlds can be expected to have undergone their equivalents of a Stone Age, Industrial Revolution, and so forth. At some point, each species would have developed the means to leave their planet and travel at [[Sublight engine/Legends|sublight]] speeds, resulting in a Space Age. The first warships appeared in this time period as well, primitive nuclear-powered rockets with basic laser weaponry.<ref name="Dark Empire Sourcebook" />

In this period, quintillions of engineering pioneers helped develop and refine the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]],<ref name="AOTC:ICS" /> following the fall of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]].<ref name="New Essential Chronology" /> Their efforts culminated in the device that would remain the predominant way of facilitating interstellar travel for over 25,000 years.<ref name="AOTC:ICS" />

[[File:Hyperspace-SWPuzzle.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The hyperdrive made interstellar travel possible.]]

The discovery of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel was the first step towards the eventual unification of the galaxy by providing species with a means to break the light barrier. The first technology to utilize this was the [[hyperspace cannon]], a device that accelerated ships into hyperspace. It had severe limitations, however: the amount of travel possible was limited by the number of cannons, a ship needed to induce drag to revert from hyperspace at the proper time, and most importantly, it was a one-way trip unless another cannon was constructed at the other end. Such cannons first connected the Humans and the Duros.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

The peak of Pre-Republic innovation occurred with the invention of the hyperdrive, which finally allowed civilizations to freely roam the galaxy by traveling many times faster than the speed of light. It was the hyperdrive that gave rise to the Galactic Republic by connecting and uniting species from worlds across the galaxy. The invention of the hyperdrive itself is sometimes attributed to the mysterious race known as the Celestials, who are said to have given it to the other species, though it is now thought that [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] and Duros scientists developed it by reverse engineering Rakatan technology.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

[[File:Hypergate.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A [[Grand Hypergate]] created by the ancient [[Gree (species)/Legends|Gree]]]]

By the time of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], myths of the Pre-Republic era included "dark knights" who fought mounted with lances, and would later be compared to the lancer troops that fought the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]]. Whether such dark knights actually existed or were just widely accepted folklore is unknown.<ref name="New Essential Chronology" />

One of the most notable developments of this period, as measured by the far-reaching effects it would come to have on galactic society, was the creation of [[Artificial intelligence/Legends|artificial intelligence]] and the invention of [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Droids had appeared in advanced civilizations for uncounted millennia, but they were first developed in what would become Republic space circa [[30,000 BBY]].<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> Some were doubtful that this technology could ever match their own natural sentience, and their doubts were echoed by noted philosophers such as [[Plaristes]].<ref name="Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena article">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}}</ref>

===Notable achievements===
====The Corellian system====
By precise control of gravitational fields, the species known as the Celestials altered the orbits of the planets in the Corellian system, resulting in the unusual case of five planets in the same system capable of sustaining life. To aid in the construction of the system, enormous [[planetary repulsor]]s were installed beneath the surface of the five inhabited planets.<ref name="Assault at Selonia">''[[Assault at Selonia]]''</ref>

[[File:Centerpoint_Station.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Centerpoint Station]]

=====Centerpoint Station=====
{{Main|Centerpoint Station}}
The incredible feats performed in the construction of the Corellian system were made possible by the enormous space station called Centerpoint, which sat at the L<sub>1</sub> point between [[Talus/Legends|Talus]] and [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]]. Centerpoint's abilities were due to its massive [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor-beam]] installation, which was even capable of moving objects through hyperspace.<ref name="Coruscant and the Core Worlds" /> In later years, the Killiks would purport to have built Centerpoint; however, there is no evidence of this other than these claims.<ref name="The Joiner King">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref> It had been speculated that the Killiks were used for manual labor by the Celestials to build the station, and that its purpose was to imprison the being known as [[Abeloth]].<ref name="Abyss">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Abyss]]''</ref>

====The Maw====
{{Main|Maw Cluster/Legends}}
In a similar manner to the Corellian system, the cluster of black holes known as the Maw was so precisely arranged that it too was likely the product of an ancient civilization. Similar gravitational technology was no doubt used to move the star-like masses of the black holes that composed the Maw.<ref name="The Jedi Academy Sourcebook">''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]''</ref> The Killiks claimed to have created it as well, but as with Centerpoint, there is no evidence to support their claim.<ref name="The Joiner King" /> Some theorized that the Maw's purpose was to serve as the prison for [[Abeloth]].<ref name="Abyss" />

====The Star Forge====
{{Main|Star Forge}}
The Star Forge was the pride of the Rakatan Infinite Empire, an enormous space station and factory that turned the raw plasma of Lehon's sun into ships and weapons. The Star Forge was a tool of the dark side, and in turn corrupted the Rakatan people further. When the Infinite Empire collapsed, the Star Forge was abandoned, its secrets locked within the [[Temple of the Ancients]]. In worlds across the galaxy, Rakatan temples held their [[Star Map]]s, built as testaments to the power of the Infinite Empire.<ref name="KOTOR" />

====Star Temples====
The [[Star Temple]]s were artifacts of the Kwa, built to house the power of the Infinity Gates. The Gates themselves were primarily a means of transportation between different worlds, although as a last resort they could also be activated as weapons that destroyed everything in their vicinity. The Star Temples protected the power of the Gates from being used by outsiders.<ref name="Infinity's End" />

==Terminology==
The term ''Pre-Republic'' was occasionally used by individuals such as Jedi Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] to refer to only the short period between the discovery of the hyperdrive and the founding of the Republic.<ref name="Power of the Jedi Sourcebook">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref> The end of the Pre-Republic era was known as the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Expansionist Era]].<ref name="Han Solo and the Lost Legacy">''[[Han Solo and the Lost Legacy]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Concepts that belong to this era use standard Earth terminology such as [[Kossak Inijic Ar'durv|Kossak]] and [[Crimson Condottiere|Condottiere]]. The word "[[Droid/Legends|robot]]" and the suffix "-bot" are preferred over "droid" to refer to Pre-Republic machines. [[Sleeper ship/Legends|Sleeper ships]] are also common in science-fiction works having to do with possible future Earth technology.

''[[Alien Exodus]]'' was a planned novel that would have been set in the Pre-Republic era, but since it was never published, the story developments that were planned for it remain non-canon.
